content.full_text:
Farn Pourrcal PnacrrcEs CourrarssroN
428 J Street o Suite 620 o Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 o Fax (916) 322-0886
January 12,2005
Carol Thompson
Future Media
2594 Emma Drive
Pinole, CA94564
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-0+263
DearMs. Thompson:
This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the conflict-of-
interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the'Act').r
QUESTION
You ask whether, as a member of the Pinole Planning Commission, you may bid
on city contracts for your wholly-owned graphic design business.
CONCLUSION
The Act's conflict of interest rules do notprohibit you from bidding on city
contracts while holding office on theplanning commission. You should, however, seek
advice from your city attorney or from the Attorney General's office on otherbodies of
law that may govern your contractual relations with the city.
FACTS
You are an appointed member of the Pinole Planning Commission, and have been
told that you cannot bid on any graphic design work with the citybecause entering into a
contractual relationship with the city, whether for graphic design ofnewsletters, signage
I Government Code sections 81000 - g1}l4. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.


File No. A-04-263
Page No. 2
for the centennial, even stationery for the Pinole Youth Commission, would be viewed as
a conflict of interest. You are the sole proprietor of a graphic design business, and do not
have any employees.
ANALYSIS
The Act's conflict of interest rules prohibit a public official from making,
participating in making, or using his or her official position in any way to influence a
goveflrmental decision in which the officiat knows, or has reason to know, that he or she
has a "financial interest." (Section S7100.) Section 87103 provides that apublic official
has a o'financial interest" in a governmental decision "ifit isreasonably foreseeable that
the decision will have a material financial effect, distinguishable fromlts effect on the
public generally, on the official, a member of his or her immediate fatnily," or on any of
the official's economic interests.
As an appointed member of the Pinole Planning Commission, you are a .fublic
official" as defined by section 82048, subject as such to the Act's conflict of interest
provisions. Under the Act a public official may only have a "conflict of interest,, when
the official has a financial interest in a governmental decision. The Act's conflict of
interest rules do not limit a public official's non-public employment or, in general, an
official's contractual dealings with governmental agencies -unless theofficial makes,
participates in making, or uses his or her official position to influence a governmental
decision that involves those contractual arrangements. You have not indicated that you
anticipate any govemmental decision coming before the planning commission regarding
a corlmercial relationship between your business and the city but, if such a decision were
presented, you could satisff the Act's conflict of interest rules simply by not making
participating in making, or using your official position to influence that decision.
However, there are other bodies oflaw separate and apart from theAct,s conflict
of interest provisions which may apply to your situation. Yow account suggests that you
may have been advised about the possible application of Govemment Code section lti90,
which generally provides that an officer or employee of any governmental agency may
not make a contract in which the officer or employee has a financial interest. Unlike 
-
provisions of the Act, which permit recusal from pertinent desisions, a board member is .
conclusively presumed under section 1090 to have made any contract executed by the
board on which he or she sits. Generally, any contract made in violation of section 1090
is void and cannot be enforced. In addition, an official who violates section 1090 maybe
subject to serious criminal, civil, and administrative sanctions.
The Commission is not permitted to offer advice on bodies of law outside the
confines of the Act, and we therefore strongly recornmend that you consult your city
attomey or the Attorney General's office regarding the possible application of section
1090 to any contract you may consider entering with the city.


File No. A-04-263
Page No. 3
If you have other questions on this matter,please contact me at (916) 322-5660
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel
br4J.*U,/
By: Lawrence T. Woodlock
Senior Counsel, I-egal Division
